title: Share of cancer deaths attributed to risk factors
subtitle: The estimated share of deaths from [cancers](#dod:cancer) attributed to all [risk factors](#dod:risk-factor). The remaining share represents deaths that would be expected to have occurred in the absence of these known risk factors.
caption:
Share of cancer deaths attributed to risk factors

The estimated share of deaths from cancers attributed to all risk factors. The remaining share represents deaths that would be expected to have occurred in the absence of these known risk factors.

This horizontal bar chart displays the estimated share of deaths from various cancer types attributed to all risk factors globally in 2023. The data is sorted in descending order, with teal bars representing the percentage for each specific cancer type. Cervical cancer sits at the top with a value of 100%, indicating that all estimated deaths from this cancer are attributed to known risk factors. Mesothelioma follows closely at 91.7%. Lung, tracheal and bronchus cancer shows a share of 76.4%, while Larynx cancer is at 73.4%. A middle cluster of cancer types includes Esophageal (56.4%), Colon and rectum (56.2%), and Liver (48.2%). Further down the list, Lip and oral cavity cancer is at 39.4%, Pancreatic at 38.7%, and Uterine at 33.3%. Bladder and Breast cancers show shares of 33% and 29.8% respectively. Kidney cancer is at 27.9%, followed by Nasopharynx at 23.7%. Stomach cancer stands at 19%, with Leukemia at 18.7%. Thyroid cancer is at 11.6%, and Gallbladder and biliary tract cancer is at 11.4%. Multiple myeloma shows a share of 7.6%, Non-hodgkin lymphoma is at 4.8%, and Prostate cancer is at the bottom with 0.9%. The note accompanying the chart states that in some cases, the IHME methodology for calculating risks can include negative values, which can reflect potential protective effects or indicate uncertainty due to weak or poorly understood relationships between risk factors and health outcomes.
